Biography ( जीवनी ) - a written account of someone’s life. Geography ( भूगोल ) - the study of the Earth’s surface, physical features, and human-environment interaction.

questions.question: The humorous or mildly sarcastic use of words to imply the opposite of what they normally mean
questions.solution: Irony ( विडंबना ) - the humorous or mildly sarcastic use of words to imply the opposite of what they normally mean. Sarcasm ( व्यंग्य ) - the use of irony to mock or convey contempt. Satire ( कटाक्ष ) - the use of humour, irony, exaggeration, or ridicule to expose and criticise people's stupidity or vices, particularly in the context of contemporary politics and other topical issues. Paradox ( विरोधाभास ) - a seemingly absurd or contradictory statement or proposition which when investigated may prove to be well founded or true.

questions.question: A person who believes that all events are ultimately determined by causes external to the will
questions.solution: Fatalist ( भाग्यवादी ) - a person who believes that all events are predetermined and therefore inevitable. Nihilist ( शून्यवादी ) - a person who believes that life is meaningless and rejects all religious and moral principles. Idealist ( आदर्शवादी ) - a person who is guided by ideals and principles rather than by practical considerations. Hedonist ( सुखवादी ) - a person who believes that the pursuit of pleasure is the most important thing in life.

questions.question: ‘A person who deliberately sets fire to property’.
questions.solution: Arsonist ( आग लगाने वाला ) - a person who intentionally sets fire to buildings or property. Pyromaniac ( दहनोन्‍माद संबंधी ) - a person who has an uncontrollable desire or mental disorder that makes them set fires for excitement. Vandal ( तोड़फोड़ करने वाला / विध्वंसक ) - a person who deliberately damages public or private property. Saboteur ( विध्वंसक ) - a person who destroys or damages something deliberately, especially for political or military advantage.

questions.question: ‘A person who believes that all events are predetermined and inevitable’.
questions.solution: Fatalist ( भाग्यवादी ) - a person who believes that all events are predetermined and inevitable. Optimist ( आशावादी ) - a person who tends to see the positive side of things and expects good outcomes. Pedant ( रूढ़िवादी ) - a person who overemphasizes rules or minor details, often in learning or teaching. Realist ( यथार्थवादी ) - a person who accepts things as they are and deals with facts rather than ideas or emotions.

questions.question: ‘A government ruled by a king or queen’ .
questions.solution: Monarchy ( राजशाही / राजतंत्र ) - a government ruled by a king or queen. Democracy ( लोकतंत्र ) - a system of government where power rests with the people, usually through elected representatives. Oligarchy ( कुलीनतंत्र ) - a government ruled by a small group of people. Autocracy ( एकतंत्र / तानाशाही ) - a system of government ruled by a single person with absolute power.

questions.question: A secret plot or conspiracy, especially one intended to overthrow a government or leader.
questions.solution: Cabal ( साजिश ) - a secret plot or conspiracy, especially one intended to overthrow a government or leader. Coup ( तख्तापलट ) - a sudden, violent, and illegal seizure of power from a government. Mutiny ( विद्रोह ) - an open rebellion against lawful authority, especially by soldiers or sailors against their officers. Machination ( षड्यंत्र ) - a crafty or secret scheme, usually intended to achieve an evil purpose.

questions.question: A person who is excessively concerned with minor details and rules.
questions.solution: Pedant ( रूढ़िवादी ) - a person who is excessively concerned with minor details and rules. Altruist ( परोपकारी ) - a person unselfishly concerned for or devoted to the welfare of others. Philistine ( संस्कृतिहीन ) - a person who is hostile or indifferent to culture and the arts, or who has no understanding of them. Dilettante ( शौकिया ) - a person who cultivates an area of interest, such as the arts, without real commitment or knowledge.

questions.question: ‘A person who pretends to have virtues, moral or religious beliefs he does not possess’
questions.solution: Hypocrite ( पाखंडी ) - a person who pretends to have virtues, moral or religious beliefs he does not possess. Charlatan ( धोखेबाज ) - a person who falsely claims to have special knowledge or skills. Fraud ( धोखाधड़ी ) - a person who deceives others for personal gain. Indolent ( आलसी ) - a person who is habitually lazy.

questions.question: ‘A person who cannot be satisfied’
questions.solution: Insatiable ( अतृप्त ) - Impossible to satisfy; having an unquenchable desire. Contented ( तृप्त / संतुष्ट ) - Pleased or satisfied with what one has; not wanting more. Avaricious ( लोभी / लालची ) - Extremely greedy, especially for wealth. Sanguine ( आशावादी ) - Optimistic or positive, especially in a difficult situation.

questions.solution: Sadist ( परपीड़क ) - a person who finds joy in the pain or suffering of others. Pessimist ( निराशावादी ) - a person who tends to see the worst aspect of things. questions.question: A statement that appears self-contradictory but contains a deeper truth.

Riddle ( उलझन भरा प्रश्न ) - a question or statement intentionally phrased so as to require ingenuity in ascertaining its answer or meaning. Puzzle ( पहेली ) - a game, toy, or problem designed to test ingenuity or knowledge.

questions.solution: Ascetic ( तपस्वी ) - a person who chooses to live a simple life, often search on TG -@freepdfhall for religious or spiritual reasons, abstaining from worldly pleasures. questions.question: 'An expression of excessive pride or self-importance.'
questions.solution: All are correct Arrogance ( अहंकार ) - an attitude of superiority and overbearing pride. Hubris ( अहंकार ) - excessive pride or self-confidence, often leading to downfall. Vanity ( घमंड ) - excessive pride in one’s appearance or achievements.

questions.solution: Aristocracy ( कुलीनतंत्र ) - a form of government in which power is held by the nobility. Monarchy ( राजतंत्र ) - a form of government with a monarch at the head.
